ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Of Meletius Bishop of Antioch.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: It becomes us now to speak of Meletius, who, as we have recently observed, was created bishop of Sebastia in Armenia, after the deposition of Eustathius; from Sebastia he was transferred to Berœa, a city of Syria.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Being present at the Synod of Seleucia, he subscribed the creed set forth there by Acacius, and immediately returned thence to Berœa.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: When the convention of the Synod at Constantinople was held, the people of Antioch finding that Eudoxius, captivated by the magnificence of the see of Constantinople, had contemned their church, they sent for Meletius, and invested him with the bishopric of the church at Antioch.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Now he at first avoided all doctrinal questions, confining his discourses to moral subjects; but subsequently he expounded to his auditors the Nicene creed, and asserted the doctrine of the homoousion.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The emperor being informed of this, ordered that he should be sent into exile; and caused Euzoïus, who had before been deposed together with Arius, to be installed bishop of Antioch in his stead.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Such, however, as were attached to Meletius, separated themselves from the Arian congregation, and held their assemblies apart: nevertheless, those who originally embraced the homoousian opinion would not communicate with them, because Meletius had been ordained by the Arians, and his adherents had been baptized by them.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Thus was the Antiochian church divided, even in regard to those whose views on matters of faith exactly corresponded.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Meanwhile the emperor getting intelligence that the Persians were preparing to undertake another war against the Romans, repaired in great haste to Antioch.
